name: Toluene/BTEX sensor (XylR + CRISPRi)
shortDescription: Detects toluene and related BTEX aromatic pollutants. The native XylR/Pu regulator gates a CRISPRi circuit driving a visible pigment, in P. putida KT2440 — a natural toluene-degrader and rugged environmental chassis.

input.detects: Toluene / benzene-toluene-ethylbenzene-xylene contamination in soil and groundwater

sensing.sensorMechanism: XylR binds toluene/m-xylene effectors and activates the Pu promoter (sigma-54 dependent).
sensing.signalFlow: Toluene -> XylR activates Pu -> transcribes an anti-pigment sgRNA -> dCas9 + sgRNA repress a constitutive amilCP cassette (CRISPRi) -> pigment fades with toluene (NOT); pair an inverter for colour-on.

parts.name: XylR regulator
parts.notes: Toluene/xylene-responsive sigma-54 activator; native effector range in P. putida.

performance.notes: XylR/Pu is a classic, well-characterised aromatic sensor native to P. putida; the CRISPRi integration is a design template.

safety.biocontainment: P. putida KT2440 is a certified HV1 safety host; add a containment circuit for field use.
safety.notes: Ideal match: P. putida natively tolerates and metabolises aromatics, so it works in real contaminated samples.

buildSteps.title: Design anti-pigment sgRNA
buildSteps.description: Target the amilCP promoter; check host off-targets.

buildSteps.title: Assemble units
buildSteps.description: TU1: XylR. TU2: Pu -> sgRNA. TU3: dCas9 + constitutive amilCP. Clone into SEVA vectors.

buildSteps.title: Transform P. putida KT2440
buildSteps.description: Electroporate; select; confirm baseline pigment without toluene.

buildSteps.title: Expose to sample
buildSteps.description: Add soil eluate / groundwater + toluene standard curve.

provenance.source: Design template combining the native XylR/Pu toluene-sensing module with P. putida CRISPRi
partsSources: Ramos et al. / TOL plasmid XylR-Pu literature (toluene sensing in P. putida)
provenance.license: Parts per their original sources; design template CC BY 4.0
